{{Infobox construct
| name = Remnant
| introduced = Unsouled
}}

''See also: [[Remnant Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Remnants]] are living forces composed of [[pure madra]] that emerge from the corpses of powerful [[sacred artists]] or certain [[sacred beasts]] upon their death. They often appear as colorful, skeletal outlines or sketches of their former physical bodies rather than detailed replicas, looking much like brushstrokes painted onto reality. While they retain fragments of their previous lives' memories and techniques, their intelligence and reasoning are typically narrow in scope. They do not possess physical bodies but can interact with the world, sometimes appearing disoriented or behaving unpredictably upon birth. These entities can vary significantly in power and appearance, ranging from small, simple spirits to massive, city-sized constructs. Higher-level [[Remnants]], such as those from [[Heralds]], appear more lifelike and robust. They can be used as resources; [[Soulsmiths]] often dissect them to harvest valuable components like bindings or specific [[madra]] aspects. Some individuals even integrate [[Remnants]] into their own bodies, such as using them to form specialized limbs or weapons. While some [[Remnants]] can be bound or even develop their own awareness over time, they are generally considered shadows of the individuals they once were.}}

== Unsouled ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] are substances or items that [[Soulsmiths]] can mold, consisting of [[pure madra]] constructions freed from their physical forms. These entities are created when a powerful [[sacred artist]] dies, leaving behind their soul as a living force that can develop consciousness and hunger for human [[madra]]. While they possess only narrow, fragmented memories of their former lives, these shadow-like beings can advance in power over time. Different forms exist, such as a purple, skeletal tree-Remnant or an ogre-shaped outline made of [[Mountain's Heart]] power. In combat, a [[sword]]-aspect Remnant provides great strength despite being weak in pursuit, and some highly powerful entities composed of [[Forged madra]] have even been known to break free from ancient seals.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] are substances or items that can be molded by [[Soulsmiths]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c1">''Unsouled'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] are constructions of [[pure madra]] freed from their physical bodies.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2">''Unsouled'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|When a [[sacred artist]] of enough power dies, they leave their soul behind as a living force known as a [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The tree-[[Remnant]] is a purple, skeletal construction of [[madra]] shaped like a leafless tree.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] hunger for pure human [[madra]] and can develop consciousness if they consume it.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The fruit shines with the bright color of a [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Breaking a sacred tree can release a [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3">''Unsouled'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] can advance over time like humans and [[sacred beasts]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16">''Unsouled'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] have only fragments of their memories from [[life]] and are narrow in scope.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] are described as shadows.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|A [[Remnant]] can be made of [[Mountain's Heart]] power and appear like an outline of an ogre painted in dirt.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c17">''Unsouled'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Taking in a [[Remnant]] is the difference between [[Jade]] and [[Gold]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|A [[Remnant]] of the [[sword]] aspect is powerful in combat but weak in pursuit.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c18">''Unsouled'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|A [[Remnant]] of an expert beyond [[Gold]] was thought to be sealed in the ancient tomb but had cut through its restraints.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c19">''Unsouled'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Remnant]] is composed of [[Forged madra]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Remnant]] leaks silvery motes of [[essence]] during battle.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c19" />}}

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are creatures or objects found within the Wilds that tend to gather in areas where [[vital aura]] is thick. Appearing as lines of color painted onto the world, these entities are often disoriented upon birth and can even emerge from walls or peel themselves out of a person's body. They gain intelligence and power by consuming human [[madra]], though they can be restricted by script circles or have their strength devoured by the [[Jai spear]]. While some [[sacred artists]] like [[Kral]] possess tiny [[Remnants]] on their bodies, others such as [[Yerin]] carry theirs wrapped around their waists like a belt, flickering with [[blood madra]]. These tricky parts of [[sacred beasts]] can be dissected into functional components, and a master may even leave behind a Remnant of powerful [[madra]]. Ultimately, a [[sacred artist]] must take on the power of a Remnant to advance from the [[Jade stage]] to [[Gold]], at which point the entity can be held within a core.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A [[Remnant]] can be held in a core at the [[Gold stage]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c2">''Soulsmith'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Yerin's Remnant]] is wrapped around her waist like a belt.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c3">''Soulsmith'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Remnant]] flickers with [[blood madra]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are often disoriented when they are born.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] look as though they have been painted onto the world in lines of color.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are creatures encountered by [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c5">''Soulsmith'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are creatures or objects found in the Wilds.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c6">''Soulsmith'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Jai spear]] devours the strength of [[Remnants]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7">''Soulsmith'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|To advance from [[Jade]] to [[Gold]], a [[sacred artist]] must take on the power of a [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A [[Remnant]] can be dissected into its functional components.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c10">''Soulsmith'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A [[Remnant]]'s corpse can be seen as unusual in a camp of [[sacred artists]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A [[Remnant]] can peel itself out of a person's body.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c11">''Soulsmith'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] gain power and intelligence by consuming human [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13">''Soulsmith'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Kral]] has a tiny [[Remnant]] on his arm.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A master can leave behind a [[Remnant]] of powerful [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c15">''Soulsmith'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are parts of [[sacred beasts]] that can be tricky to handle.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] attack [[Lindon]] regularly.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16">''Soulsmith'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] can be restricted by script circles.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c17">''Soulsmith'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are creatures that gather where [[vital aura]] is thick.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] can emerge from walls.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c18">''Soulsmith'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A Remnant is a body composed of solid [[madra]] that lacks muscle, appearing instead as a collection of brush-strokes or a constellation of bright light points forming joints, eyes, and a heart. These entities represent a stage of progression higher than an [[Iron]], possessing [[madra]] that [[dreadbeasts]] can consume and which can be broken down to provide excess energy or digested for skills. During an encounter, a tall, sunken Remnant with long limbs and a fish-like face attacked [[Lindon]] using purple madra that adhered to his ankle like silk. While powerful individuals can swat them away like flies, a strong enough suction force in the core can contain one. Some [[Remnants]], such as the one with sapphire eyes found in [[Lindon]]'s pack, possess the power to cleanse and restore [[madra]], and they may even leave techniques or powers behind for a successor.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A [[Remnant]] is a body without muscle to cut through.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1">''Blackflame'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Remnants]] contain [[madra]] that [[dreadbeasts]] can eat.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Remnants]] look like a collection of brush-strokes.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Remnants]] can be dissected using specialized tools.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A [[Remnant]] attacked [[Lindon]] with purple madra that stuck to his ankle like spider's silk.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Remnant]] that attacked [[Lindon]] was tall and sunken with long limbs and a fish-like face.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A [[Remnant]] can be swatted like a fly by someone powerful enough.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai Long]] has a [[Remnant]] in his core that is blending with his own [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c2">''Blackflame'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A [[Remnant]] can leave behind techniques or powers to a successor.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c4">''Blackflame'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Remnant]] looked like a constellation of bright light points forming joints, hands, eyes, and a heart.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Remnant]]'s roar sounded like a bonfire.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A [[Remnant]] is a stage of progression higher than an [[Iron]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c5">''Blackflame'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Remnants]] are made of solid [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c6">''Blackflame'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A strong enough suction force in the core can contain a [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A [[Remnant]] can be broken down for power by digesting its skills and [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c7">''Blackflame'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A [[Remnant]] can provide excess energy.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin]] has a death-grip on her [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13">''Blackflame'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Remnant]] in [[Lindon]]'s pack has sapphire eyes.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21">''Blackflame'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Remnant]]'s power can cleanse and restore [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21" />}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Remnant]] limbs can retain their own [[life]] and will.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7">''Skysworn'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The arm is a [[Remnant]]'s arm.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c12">''Skysworn'', chapter 12</ref>}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|A Remnant is a prosthetic limb composed of light. During an explosion, a Remnant made of black clouds launched itself forward. These entities can rise from a body in the form of a silver-blue light that resembles a wire model of the original creature. In one instance, a Remnant was observed attempting to rise from a new body.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|A [[Remnant]] is a prosthetic limb made of light.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c1">''Ghostwater'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|A [[Remnant]] of black clouds launched itself forward after an explosion.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c3">''Ghostwater'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|A [[Remnant]] can rise from a body as a silver-blue light.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c8">''Ghostwater'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|A [[Remnant]] looks like a wire model of the original creature.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|A [[Remnant]] was attempting to rise from a new body.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c8" />}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Remnants]] manifest in diverse and surreal forms, such as a large silver and purple book featuring a single eye that appeared behind the [[Sage]]. Some entities take on animalistic shapes, including a corpse resembling a rainbow-colored donkey that bleeds multicolored [[essence]]. Other instances involve the provision of [[essence]] by a dying Remnant within an underground chamber or the appearance of a hulking, armored giant with metallic flesh and a smooth boulder for a face. Additionally, parts of these beings can be integrated into others, as seen when [[Lindon]] possesses a white Remnant limb.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|An apparition behind the [[Sage]] appeared like a [[Remnant]], manifesting as a large silver and purple book with a [[Remnant]] eye.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4">''Underlord'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|A dying [[Remnant]] provides [[essence]] in an underground chamber.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5">''Underlord'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|A [[Remnant]]'s corpse can look like a donkey fashioned of rainbows and bleed multicolored [[essence]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c14">''Underlord'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Lindon]] has a white [[Remnant]] limb.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c17">''Underlord'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|A [[Remnant]] appeared as a hulking figure like an armored giant with a smooth boulder for a face and metallic [[madra]] flesh.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c20">''Underlord'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Remnant]] pieces do not always combine as expected.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c8">''Uncrowned'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Remnants]] are used in the [[Everwood continent]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c13">''Uncrowned'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A Remnant is a [[construct]] or entity that can be forged using [[madra]]. These entities can be assembled from remnants of force, earth, or [[sword madra]] to create objects such as training dummies. A Remnant can also rise from a corpse, inheriting the thoughts of the person from whom it emerged. While powerful, an [[Overlord]]'s Remnant can be destroyed through the application of spiritual damage.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The training dummy is assembled from [[Remnants]] of [[sword]], earth, or [[force madra]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1">''Wintersteel'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Remnant]] can rise from a person's body and inherit their thoughts.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Remnant]] is a [[construct]] or entity that can be forged from [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Remnant]] can rise from a corpse.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|An [[Overlord]]'s [[Remnant]] can be destroyed by spiritual damage.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c27">''Wintersteel'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Sacred artists can take [[Remnants]] into their spirit.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6">''Reaper'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Beings stronger than Archlords do not often leave [[Remnants]] when they die.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13">''Reaper'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Archlady]] restored her [[Remnant]] after being struck.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Remnant]] [[Lindon]] strengthened for [[Kelsa]] gave her denser and better balanced [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Remnants]] are notoriously unreliable.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Appearing as a mute, nightmare-like skeleton within [[Yerin]]'s classroom, this Remnant serves as a leash for her. As echoes of people from their former lives, these beings consume pieces of themselves to function. After the death of [[Malice]], the entity rose amidst a dim purple light and beamed encouragement at [[Mercy]] to prevent her from giving up. Though it once existed as a lion, the Remnant has transitioned into a man-like form and possesses the capability to be integrated into a person's body.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Remnant]] can act as a leash for [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Remnant]] is a mute nightmare skeleton in [[Yerin]]'s classroom.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12">''Waybound'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Remnants]] are echoes of people from their lives.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c18">''Waybound'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Remnants]] use up pieces of themselves as they work.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Remnant]] beamed at [[Mercy]] and encouraged her not to give up.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c26">''Waybound'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A dim purple light was the [[Remnant]] rising after [[Malice]]'s death.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c28">''Waybound'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Remnant]] can be integrated into a person's body.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29">''Waybound'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Remnant]] is a being that once was a lion but now looks like a man.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c33">''Waybound'', chapter 33</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|“Good,” Kral said, and the tiny Remnant on his arm bit down.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|“You're an Iron, not a Remnant; you still need sleep. Food.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|“Her madra cleanses and restores, I think. She helped me too.”<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The Remnant beamed. “Don’t give up, Mercy! We’ll do this together!”<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c26" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Constructs]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
